text.title: Business Process Analysis Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every business-process-analysis cycle enforces.

rule.statement: Every documented step cites its source: file path + line / ticket id / transcript timestamp. Steps without source are removed from the model.
rule.rationale: Uncited steps are folklore. Source citation forces evidence over assumption.

rule.statement: Every swimlane labels a NAMED owner (full name + role), not a group ("Ops Team"). Lanes without a named owner block the cycle.
rule.rationale: Anonymous lanes hide accountability gaps and produce vague requirements downstream.

rule.statement: The analysis stage produces a table with columns: step, value-add (Y/N), cycle-time-minutes, cost-usd. Skipping any column fails the stage.
rule.rationale: Without quantification, redesign decisions reduce to opinion.

rule.statement: Future state is expressed as a diff table (step, action ∈ {keep, cut, change, add}, expected-delta) — not prose. Each row references the current-state step id.
rule.rationale: Prose future-state hides which current steps survive. A diff is auditable and machine-readable.

rule.statement: Each lane owner signs off in writing (PR comment, email, Slack ack with timestamp) on both current and future state before report ships.
rule.rationale: Unvalidated future-states get rejected at rollout. Written sign-off catches divergence early and protects the BA against scope-creep allegations.

example.title: Diff row that satisfies r4
example.code: | step_id | action | expected_delta | |---------|--------|----------------| | s-04 | cut | -120 min cycle, -$18 cost | | s-05 | change | replace manual entry with n8n webhook; -90 min | | s-09 | add | new compliance checkpoint; +5 min, +$0 |
example.note: Each row references current-state step + carries a quantified delta.

example.title: Prose future-state that fails r4
example.code: "We will automate the invoice handling and remove manual steps."
example.note: No step ids, no quantified delta, untestable.
